is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum DHFR genotypes in Malian children.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 108 (Asn), 51 (Ile), 59 (Arg); also Pfdhps 436 (Ser) and 437 (Gly).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Baseline village-wide n=119: 12.8% Asn-108, 4.2% Ile-51, 10.9% Arg-59; breakthrough n=52: 100% Asn-108, 50% Ile-51, 90% Arg-59 (Figure 1).

sub_country_location.evidence: Tieneguebougou, a rural village ~30 km northeast of Bamako, Mali — a specific named site below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: 'The study was conducted during the 1996 rainy season' — a single collection year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field samples collected from children in Tieneguebougou village, Mali, an African country.

is_primary_study.evidence: Prospective empirical study with PCR genotyping of field infections.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-village prospective collection; no mention of consortium or shared dataset, though authors have related Mali studies (ref 9).

supplement.evidence: Frequencies given in main text and Figure 1; no external supplement required.

assessment.summary: Prospective study in Tieneguebougou village, Mali (1996 rainy season) measuring pyrimethamine prophylaxis selection of Pfdhfr mutations in 109 children. Reports extractable Pfdhfr 108/51/59 frequencies at baseline (n=119) and in breakthrough infections (n=52); also detected Pfdhps 436/437. Site-level location and single collection year satisfy resolution criteria.
